project_manifest.name: Universal Office
project_manifest.operational_status: OPERATIONAL — v2.9.4: Horologion v1.8 complete. Sunday Small Vespers now implemented as a baseline path, distinct from Saturday Great Vespers. All five original Sunday-case variable slots resolved: stichera-at-lord-i-have-cried and aposticha use the resurrectional tone corpus routed through the Saturday Octoechos data with explicit Sunday Small Vespers labeling; kathisma-reading resolves to an actionable omission rubric (Sunday Small Vespers ordinarily omits kathisma); troparion-or-apolytikion resolves to the full Resurrectional Troparion of the tone (type:'text'); theotokion-dismissal resolves to the full dismissal Theotokion of the tone (type:'text'). Every original Vespers variable slot now yields full text, an explicit rubric stub, or an explicit omission rubric across all day-of-week paths. v1.9: weekday Vespers dismissal Theotokion now materially resolved for Mon–Fri. New file data/horologion/vespers-weekday-theotokion.json (40 entries: 8 tones × 5 weekdays). Engine _resolveTheotokionSlot() weekday branch upgraded from tone-identified rubric stub to type:'text' full resolution. Monday/Tuesday/Thursday: standard Octoechos weekday Theotokion for tone + day. Wednesday/Friday: Stavrotheotokion (Cross Theotokion) — standard Byzantine fasting-day practice. resolvedAs:'weekday-octoechos-theotokion'. Limitation recorded in output: baseline uses current Octoechos tone; Menaion troparion tone may differ (festal override not implemented). office-ui.js: NO CHANGES. Remaining refinement work: (a) weekday Menaion refinement — troparion text for Mon–Fri requires Menaion data; currently tone-identified stub; theotokion is now resolved from Octoechos; (b) feast-rank override system — proper feast apolytikia and Theotokia require feast-rank engine; (c) later layers: Great Lent variable overrides, kathisma full psalm text import. Prior: v1.8 Sunday Small Vespers baseline; v1.7 theotokion-dismissal baseline; v1.6 troparion baseline; v1.5 kathisma; v1.4 Mon–Fri Octoechos text; v1.3 tone engine + Saturday stichera; v1.2 prokeimenon + vesperal-reading; v1 architecture boundary.

open_bugs: 1. Rubric logic: [rite] placeholder resolution in rubrics.json is fragile and prone to null returns. (LOW — see known_outstanding_issues.rite_placeholder_resolution)
architectural_debt: 1. Heavy reliance on .innerHTML string building in office-ui.js needs transition to a template system.

2. [CLOSED — v2.8.6] Hydration-race gating: renderOffice() is guarded by isHydrationComplete. selectMode() awaits hydration before setting that flag and calling requestRender(). requestRender()/flushRender() coalesce and serialise renders via activeRender. init() is kernel-only and no longer silently triggers Daily Office hydration. Residual: no automated regression test covers this gating path.
3. Redundant liturgical fragments (Gloria Patri/Kyrie) should be normalized into common.json.

4. Saints data model: identities.json + commemorations.json are the canonical source-of-truth. js/saints-resolver.js provides the shared resolver boundary. office-ui.js and admin.html route all saints paths through SaintsResolver. Phase 4 duplicate identity consolidation complete (v2.8.8): 46 pairs resolved, 1,228 identities remain. COE audit and governance complete (v2.8.9). Remaining: normalized-source runtime adapter (SaintsResolver reads monthly cache files at runtime; a true adapter would query identities.json + commemorations.json directly).

5. [CLOSED — v2.8.9] COE saint/commemoration rendering: Three-layer rebuild complete. Layer 1 (season engine) was already operational. Layer 2 (fixed feasts: Sawma Rabba Friday martyrs, pre-Sawma Commemoration of the Faithful Departed) implemented in COE-IIA via getDayClass() in calendar-east-syriac.js. Layer 3 (sparse individual saints) source-data corrected via COE-IIB audit (133 IDs removed), eligibility gate implemented in js/coe-eligibility.js (CoeEligibility.filter()), and display re-enabled in renderEastSyriac(). saint-grid model eliminated. CoeEligibility.filter() is mandatory before any Layer 3 render. Layer 2 output from getDayClass() renders before Layer 3 lookup. No saint-grid fallback. Silence is correct when no eligible saint is present.

6. EO old/new calendar mode is partially implemented in admin-only tooling (CalendarEngine.getEOSeasonRanges() + admin/admin.html snapshots), but the main application still has no EO mode UI and does not load js/calendar-eastern-orthodox.js on the production path.
resolved_in_this_version: 1. COE saint section silenced: renderEastSyriac() saint display locked to empty pending architectural rebuild. Previous behaviour was historically inaccurate — string-matching tradition fields was surfacing wrong saints. Silence is liturgically correct for COE weekdays with no fixed saint.

resolved_in_this_version: 2. Saints resolver boundary (v2.8.5–2.8.6): js/saints-resolver.js introduced as shared module (SaintsResolver). office-ui.js and admin.html route all saints paths through it. resolveCommemorations() is now a pure delegation wrapper. OOR inline fallback filters replaced with SaintsResolver.filterCachedByTradition(). appData.saints eliminated. No direct saints-cache fetches remain outside saints-resolver.js.

3. Hydration-race gating (v2.8.6 — doc closure): The scheduler/gating layer is implemented and verified. renderOffice() returns early unless isHydrationComplete is true. selectMode() awaits the correct hydration function before setting isHydrationComplete = true and calling requestRender(). requestRender()/flushRender() coalesce concurrent requests and serialise renders via activeRender. init() is kernel-only and no longer silently triggers Daily Office hydration. The original architectural debt note is closed as implemented.

js/office-ui.js.horologion_adapter_note: Thin adapter only. renderHorologionOffice() calls HorologionEngine.resolveOffice(), checks payload.status === 'error' explicitly, renders diagnostic banner when placeholderSlots > 0, walks sections/items via _renderHorologionItem(). _renderHorologionItem() handles types: rubric, psalm (label + formatted body), litany (role-tagged lines: Deacon/Priest/Reader/Bishop → rubric-text; Choir → indented italic), text/fallback (paragraph + linebreak). selectMode('horologion-vespers') calls loadKernel() only — no tradition shard needed. No Byzantine logic lives in the UI layer.

public_methods: init() — loads bcp-propers.json; must be called before fetchLectionaryData()

getCurrentDate()
setCurrentDate(date)
changeDate(days)
resetDate()

private_methods: _getEaster(year) — Anonymous Gregorian algorithm; returns JS Date of Easter Sunday for any year
_getAdventSunday(year) — returns JS Date of Advent Sunday (Sunday on or after Nov 27) for any year
_generateSeasonRanges(year) — generates all season range objects for the liturgical year beginning in Nov/Dec of `year`
_getRangesForDate(date) — returns the correct generated range set for any date; checks prior and current calendar year

_isoFromDate(date) — local-time-safe YYYY-MM-DD formatter used by EO diagnostics

public_methods: getEthiopianDate(gregorianDate) — returns { day, month, monthIndex, year } in Amete Mihret era
getCopticDate(gregorianDate) — same algorithm, Coptic month names, EC-276 era
formatEthiopianDate(gregorianDate) — returns display string e.g. '15 Yekatit 2018'

implementation_notes: 1. Julian Day Number (JDN) method — valid across all historical and future dates
2. Epoch: 1 Meskerem 1 EC = JDN 1724221 = 29 August 8 AD (Julian)
3. Pagume: 5 days normally, 6 in leap years (year % 4 === 3)
4. Self-test suite on load — 9 verified reference points logged to console

hydration_race_condition.description: RESOLVED — v2.8.6 (doc closure). The hydration-race gating layer is implemented and verified in code. renderOffice() returns early unless isHydrationComplete is true. selectMode() awaits the correct hydration function before setting isHydrationComplete = true and calling requestRender(). requestRender() / flushRender() coalesce concurrent render requests and serialise execution via activeRender. init() is kernel-only and no longer silently triggers Daily Office hydration.

coe_calendar_model.description: RESOLVED — v2.8.9. Three-layer COE calendar model implemented. Layer 1: season engine (calendar-east-syriac.js getSeason() / getDayClass()) operational. Layer 2: getDayClass() returns commemorations[] for Sawma Rabba Fridays and pre-Sawma Faithful Departed. Layer 3: CoeEligibility.filter() allowlist gate implemented; display re-enabled in renderEastSyriac(). COE-IIB audit complete: 133 identity IDs (136 rows) removed from commemorations.json. saint-grid model eliminated. No saint-grid fallback. Layer 2 renders before Layer 3 lookup. Silence is correct when no eligible saint is present.
coe_calendar_model.architectural_principle: Latin logic: Calendar = Saints framework with seasonal overlay. East Syriac logic: Calendar = Salvation-historical seasons with occasional saints.

layer_2_fixed_feasts.description: Major fixed observances calculated seasonally, not stored as date-keyed saints.

layer_3_individual_saints.status: COMPLETE — COE-IIB audit done; CoeEligibility.filter() gate active; display re-enabled v2.8.7
layer_3_individual_saints.description: Sparse. Conservative. Person-based only. Pre-schism and explicitly East Syriac.
exclusion_criteria: Eastern Orthodox saints not in COE usage
Saints in Roman Martyrology but not in Hudra
Saints included only because they are 'early enough'
Gregory the Great — disputed, currently tagged COE, needs review
Any saint tagged COE solely by proximity to EOR
layer_3_individual_saints.action: No further internal action required. One external-research holdout remains: mar-augustine / mar-augustine-commemoration — excluded from Layer 3 by the eligibility gate. Do not alter on internal evidence alone. Resolution requires external Syriac manuscript evidence (Budge Book of the Governors, Wright Catalogue, Encyclopaedia Iranica COE calendar). If a matching Hudra figure is confirmed: correct feast dates, merge identity records, add to allowlist. If no figure is found: REMOVE_COE_TAG from both records.

season_weekly_logic.subara: Text-heavy, saint-light. Marian material doxological. No Immaculate Conception construct.
season_weekly_logic.nativity: Synaxis-type commemorations. Apostles and biblical figures in propers. Major feasts are feast/commemoration type.
season_weekly_logic.denha: Occasional fixed apostles. Strong baptismal theology. Friday commemorations continue.
season_weekly_logic.sawma_rabba: Friday = Commemoration of the Martyrs (corporate). No rotating individual saint list. Inject dynamically.
season_weekly_logic.qyamta: Apostolic figures more visible. Thomas receives liturgical prominence. Paschal weeks suppress martyr themes.
season_weekly_logic.shlihe: Apostles commemorated strongly. Missionary saints possible (pre-schism). Individual apostle entries appropriate.
season_weekly_logic.eliya_sliwa: Prophetic focus. Prophets and OT figures appropriate.

TROPARION_URL constant added. _troparionData cache variable added. _loadTroparionData(): lazy fetch, non-throwing, same pattern as existing loaders. _resolveTroparionSlot(dayOfWeek, toneResult): Bright Week → type:'text', resolvedAs:'bright-week-paschal-troparion'; Saturday → type:'text' with full Resurrectional Troparion, resolvedAs:'resurrectional-troparion-saturday'; Sunday → type:'rubric', resolvedAs:'sunday-deferred'; Mon–Fri → type:'rubric' stub, resolvedAs:'weekday-menaion-required' (tone stated, text not fabricated). All cases state their limitations openly in rendered text. Promise.all in _resolveVespersSlots: three-loader → four-loader (adds _loadTroparionData()). Slot loop: deferred comment replaced with live troparion-or-apolytikion branch; theotokion-dismissal deferred comment updated. office-ui.js: NO CHANGES — type:'text' already handled by existing final branch in _renderHorologionItem (same renderer as O Gladsome Light and Nunc Dimittis). NET: troparion-or-apolytikion no longer shows dashed placeholder on Saturday or Bright Week. Mon–Fri shows tone-identified stub (not placeholder box). Sunday shows deferred stub (not placeholder box). theotokion-dismissal: unchanged placeholder.

recent_logic_changes.change: Horologion v1.5 — kathisma-reading baseline resolved for ordinary Monday–Saturday Vespers
recent_logic_changes.detail: data/horologion/vespers-kathisma.json: new file. 7-entry appointment table keyed by JS Date.getDay() (0=Sunday … 6=Saturday). Rule: standard Byzantine Psalter weekly cycle for ordinary Vespers (OCA/Antiochian Psalter table; Jordanville Horologion 2008). Assignments (LXX psalm numbers): Monday=Kathisma 4 (Ps 24–31), Tuesday=6 (37–45), Wednesday=8 (55–63), Thursday=10 (70–76), Friday=12 (85–90, 92), Saturday=1 (1–8). Sunday entry: sunday_deferred:true with honest note about usage variation. js/horologion-engine.js: version bumped v1.3→v1.5. KATHISMA_URL constant added. _kathismaData cache variable added. _loadKathismaData(): lazy fetch, non-throwing, same pattern as existing loaders. _resolveKathismaSlot(dayOfWeek, brightWeek): Bright Week → type:'rubric', resolvedAs:'bright-week-no-kathisma'; Sunday → type:'rubric', resolvedAs:'sunday-deferred'; Mon–Sat ordinary → type:'rubric' with kathisma number, title, LXX range, incipit, resolvedAs:'ordinary-weekday-baseline'. Friday entry includes variant_note about Ps 91/92 edition differences. All three cases state their limitations openly in rendered text — no silent approximations. Promise.all in _resolveVespersSlots: two-loader → three-loader (adds _loadKathismaData()). slot loop: deferred comment replaced with live kathisma-reading branch. office-ui.js: NO CHANGES — type:'rubric' is already handled by existing renderer. NET: kathisma-reading slot no longer shows as a dashed placeholder on Mon–Sat ordinary Vespers. Sunday kathisma remains an honest stub. troparion-or-apolytikion and theotokion-dismissal: unchanged placeholders.

recent_logic_changes.change: Horologion v1.4 — Monday–Friday baseline Octoechos stichera/aposticha materially populated
recent_logic_changes.detail: data/horologion/octoechos-vespers.json: version bumped 1.3.0 → 1.4.0. Replaced baseline_stub:true entries for Monday–Friday with fully populated stichera_at_lord_i_have_cried and aposticha objects (coverage:'full-baseline') for all 8 tones. 80 weekday slots populated (8 tones × 5 days × 2 slots); 240 verses total (3 per slot). Thematic assignments per standard Byzantine Octoechos practice: Monday=Bodiless Powers (Angels), Tuesday=St. John the Baptist and the Prophets, Wednesday=Cross and Theotokos, Thursday=Holy Apostles and St. Nicholas, Friday=Cross and Theotokos (penitential). Sunday entries: updated from bare baseline_stub to sunday_deferred:true + baseline_stub:true with an explicit note explaining that Saturday great Vespers already provides the Sunday-tone resurrectional content, and Sunday small Vespers ordinary stichera are a distinct corpus deferred to a future pass. This is an explicit deferral, not a silent gap. js/horologion-engine.js: NO CHANGES in this pass. The existing _resolveSticheraSlot() three-tier logic (full data → stub → null) already handles the newly populated weekday entries without modification. office-ui.js: NO CHANGES. Saturday data: unchanged from v1.3. NET: Monday–Friday Vespers stichera and aposticha are now materially resolved for all 8 tones. Sunday remains an honest stub. kathisma-reading, troparion-or-apolytikion, theotokion-dismissal: unchanged placeholders.

recent_logic_changes.change: Horologion v1.3 — baseline Octoechos tone engine; Saturday Vespers stichera/aposticha populated; weekday tone-aware stubs
recent_logic_changes.detail: _computeOrthodoxPascha(): self-contained Meeus Julian Paschalion (duplicated from calendar-eastern-orthodox.js which is admin-only; documented for future delegation when that module reaches main path). _computeBaselineTone(): Thomas Sunday anchor (Pascha+7 = Tone 1); ((weekCount % 8) + 8) % 8 + 1 handles pre-Pascha negative counts; Saturday→Sunday correction applied; Bright Week (Pascha–Thomas Saturday) returns tone:null. _loadOctoechosData(): lazy-loads data/horologion/octoechos-vespers.json; non-throwing. _resolveSticheraSlot(): three-tier resolution — (1) full data (Saturday): type:'stichera' with rubric+verses; (2) stub (weekday): type:'rubric' with tone identified and honest deferral note; (3) load failure: null → slot remains skeleton placeholder. Bright Week branches render rubric noting Paschal Tone. office-ui.js: +11 lines adding type:'stichera' handler (label+body, mirrors psalm branch). data/horologion/octoechos-vespers.json: tones 1–8; Saturday fully populated; Mon–Fri+Sunday: baseline_stub:true entries. NET: Saturday Vespers stichera and aposticha are now materially resolved for all 8 tones. Weekday stichera/aposticha are no longer dead generic placeholders — tone is resolved and displayed — but full weekday Octoechos text remains a deferred data-pass item. kathisma-reading, troparion-or-apolytikion, theotokion-dismissal: unchanged placeholders.

recent_logic_changes.change: COE final five adjudication — 3 REMOVE_COE_TAG, 2 external-research holdout
recent_logic_changes.detail: saint-ignatius-of-antioch (COE 10/17, 12/20): removed — mechanical two-date following (LAT on 10/17, EOR/OOR on 12/20); Antioch is Syriac Orthodox jurisdiction, not COE. saint-nicholas-of-myra (COE 12/6): removed — five-tradition sweep; identity description itself says "celebrated across all traditions"; Myra geographically outside COE heartland. saint-abraham-of-carrhae (COE 2/14): removed — Harran is west of Euphrates, Roman/Byzantine territory, Antiochene jurisdiction; COE followed OOR mechanically. mar-augustine / mar-augustine-commemoration: STILL_UNRESOLVED — retained as external-research holdout. Mar- prefix is genuine East Syriac evidence but three-date structure across two identity records is architecturally abnormal; cannot adjudicate without external Syriac manuscript evidence. Excluded from Layer 3 by eligibility gate. commemorations.json: 1808 → 1804 rows. February, October, December caches regenerated.

Two surgical edits only: (1) resolveCommemorations(currentDate, "COE") cache warm-up added before office HTML assembly. (2) Silenced Layer 3 tail replaced with gated block: coeRaw → CoeEligibility.filter() → render .saint-section if eligible saints present, hide if none. CoeEligibility.filter() is mandatory — no "filter all COE-tagged" shortcut. No saint-grid fallback. Silence is correct when no eligible saint is present. Layer 2 (getDayClass() commemorations[]) renders before Layer 3 lookup. typeof CoeEligibility guard degrades silently if file fails to load.

recent_logic_changes.change: COE saint section silenced pending architectural rebuild
recent_logic_changes.detail: esySaints filter and saint-display block removed from renderEastSyriac(). saint-section hidden, saint-display cleared. Previous string-matching behaviour was historically inaccurate for the East Syriac tradition. COE calendar is salvation-historical and seasonal — not a date-to-saint grid. Correct rebuild requires three-layer model (season engine, fixed feasts, sparse saints). Full spec in known_outstanding_issues.coe_calendar_model. Silence is liturgically accurate for COE weekdays with no fixed individual saint.

recent_logic_changes.change: Saints data migration — tradition string to tags[] array schema
recent_logic_changes.detail: All 1568 saint records across 12 monthly files migrated. Legacy `tradition` string field replaced with: id (stable slug), type (saint/apostle/prophet/feast/marian_feast/commemoration), tags[] (ANG/LAT/EOR/OOR/COE), verification_status (verified/provisional). 137 formerly-Universal entries fully classified via external research and editorial review. Mechanical renames: Catholic->LAT, Anglican Communion->ANG, Eastern Orthodox->EOR, Oriental Orthodox->OOR, Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo->OOR, Byzantine Orthodox->EOR, Church of the East->COE. Multi-tradition comma-separated strings parsed and split correctly. Barbara/John of Damascus combined entry split into saint-barbara-of-nicomedia (EOR/LAT/OOR) and saint-john-of-damascus (ANG/EOR/LAT). Conception of the BVM split: Dec 8 feast-immaculate-conception (ANG/LAT) and Dec 9 feast-conception-of-the-theotokos (EOR/OOR). ECU never stored — derived dynamically at render time when all five tags present. JS filter logic in office-ui.js requires update (next task).

saints_model.status_note: All 12 monthly cache files are present as of v2.8.2 and last regenerated in v2.8.8 (Phase 4 duplicate consolidation). js/saints-resolver.js (SaintsResolver) is the sole monthly-cache owner and the canonical saints boundary. office-ui.js and admin.html route all saints paths through SaintsResolver. No appData.saints usage or direct saints-cache fetches remain outside saints-resolver.js. Phase 4 duplicate identity consolidation complete: 46 pairs resolved, 1,228 unique identities remain.
saints_model.workflow: Edit identities.json or commemorations.json → run `npm run saints:regen` (validates then regenerates all months) → commit modified source files and all regenerated monthly cache files together. To regenerate a single month: `node tools/build_saints_cache.js <month>`.
rules: Do not edit saints-<month>.json files manually.
All dates in source-of-truth files must use numeric { month, day }.
Tradition is defined only in commemorations.json.
ECU is derived by generator when identity has commemorations in all five traditions on the same date.
Generated cache records carry a `type` field derived from identity.type. Do not flatten type distinctions (saint / feast / commemoration / apostle / prophet / marian_feast) at the UI layer.
unfinished_work: Normalized-source runtime adapter: SaintsResolver reads generated cache files at runtime. A true adapter would query identities.json + commemorations.json directly.

admin._note: Admin dashboard configuration. todos are the authoritative task list consumed by the /admin UI. Completed items remain in the array as ledger history; do not delete them when reclassifying work.
admin._how_to_add_todo: Add an object to the todos array with: id (kebab-case unique), title (short), description (full), severity (HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW), status (open|in-progress|done), area (code-area or file), and optionally phase.
